desc: Leh is a high-altitude Ladakh base for monasteries, stark mountain valleys, and road-trip routes toward Nubra and Pangong, with short warm seasons and very cold winters shaping trip timing.
hls: Best weather and road access typically runs June-September; shoulder months are cooler but quieter.
Excellent for monastery circuits, dramatic viewpoints, and multi-day Himalayan drives.
High elevation (~3,500m) means acclimatization planning is essential on arrival.
Large day-night temperature swings are common even in summer; layering matters year-round.
Dry climate and strong sun make UV protection and hydration non-negotiable.
Works well as a 4-7 day stop combined with Nubra Valley or Pangong Lake extensions.

todo.name: Leh Palace + old town walk
todo.desc: Pair the Leh Palace ridge views with an unhurried walk through the old town lanes and market streets.

todo.name: Thiksey or Hemis monastery visit
todo.desc: Dedicate half a day to one major monastery for murals, assembly halls, and mountain-set architecture.

todo.name: Shanti Stupa at golden hour
todo.desc: Go near sunset for broad views over Leh and the surrounding brown-and-snow Himalayan ridges.

todo.name: Nubra Valley day/overnight
todo.desc: Cross Khardung La for dune landscapes, villages, and monasteries in a contrasting valley environment.

todo.name: Pangong Lake excursion
todo.desc: Plan a very early departure for the long but iconic high-altitude lake route and changing water colors.

todo.name: Acclimatization buffer day
todo.desc: Keep your first full day deliberately light to adapt before intense road travel or long hikes.

wifi.note: Hotels and cafés in central Leh are workable, but speeds and uptime drop noticeably on remote valley routes.

prac.fltNote: No nonstop UK service; typical routing is via Delhi with strongest fare pressure in July-August and around late-December holidays.
prac.lang: Ladakhi and Hindi widely used; English is common in tourism-facing hotels and agencies.
prac.visa: India entry rules vary by nationality; confirm visa/e-visa eligibility and any regional permit requirements before booking.
alerts: Acute mountain sickness risk is real above 3,000m—ascend gradually and watch symptoms.
Weather or military controls can temporarily close mountain roads.
Cash is still useful for smaller settlements outside central Leh.

sweet: June and September are usually the best balance of open roads, manageable crowds, and comfortable daytime conditions.
